title: Accessibility
subtitle: Mood UI components ship with ARIA semantics, keyboard navigation and visible focus management out of the box. Here is how we approach accessibility and what to expect.
disclaimerBody: Mood UI is not officially audited or certified against WCAG 2.2 AA. Conformance is assessed on a best-effort basis. Accessibility in your application also depends on how you compose components — providing labels, preserving focus, and maintaining adequate contrast in your own color overrides.
targetDesc: We design every component to meet WCAG 2.2 Level AA where applicable. This covers the four core POUR principles: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable and Robust.

contrast.title: Color contrast
contrast.text: Default color tokens meet the 4.5:1 ratio for normal text and 3:1 for large text and UI components. Custom palettes via paletteFromHex() follow the same rules.

keyboard.title: Keyboard navigation
keyboard.text: Every interactive component works without a mouse. Tab, Space, Enter, Escape and arrow keys follow established WAI-ARIA patterns.

focus.title: Focus management
focus.text: Focus is always visible. Modal, Drawer and ConfirmDialog implement a focus trap — focus stays inside the overlay and returns to the trigger on close.

aria.title: ARIA semantics
aria.text: Native HTML is preferred. ARIA roles, states and properties (dialog, combobox, aria-live, aria-expanded…) are added only when necessary.

motion.title: Reduced motion
motion.text: All transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion. When the user opts out, animations are replaced with instant state changes.

labels.title: Accessible labels
labels.text: Icon-only buttons and close buttons expose aria-label. Use the label or aria-label prop to give screen readers meaningful text.

wcagDesc: WCAG 2.2 is the W3C standard for web accessibility. Level AA is the widely accepted baseline for enterprise and public-sector software.

perceivable.label: Perceivable
perceivable.text: Information and UI must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive — text alternatives, sufficient contrast, adaptable content.

operable.label: Operable
operable.text: UI components and navigation must be fully operable — keyboard accessible, no seizure triggers, enough time to interact, visible focus.

understandable.label: Understandable
understandable.text: Content and operation must be understandable — readable text, predictable behavior, helpful error messages.

robust.label: Robust
robust.text: Content must be interpreted by current and future assistive technologies — valid semantics, name/role/value for all UI components.

nextDesc: Each component page includes an accessibility notes section with keyboard shortcuts, ARIA roles and focus behavior.
nextComponentsDesc: Every component is documented with its a11y behavior.
kbDesc: Complete keyboard shortcut reference organized by component category.

kbActions.closeOverlay: Close overlay, restore focus to trigger

ariaDesc: Role and attribute patterns used by Mood UI components to communicate semantics to assistive technologies.

ariaPatterns.dialog: Focus-trapped overlay dialog. Background is inert while open.
ariaPatterns.combobox: Dropdown selector. Communicates expanded/collapsed state to screen readers.
ariaPatterns.radiogroup: Grouped radio inputs with arrow-key navigation between them.
ariaPatterns.switch: Binary toggle control that announces on/off state.
ariaPatterns.slider: Numeric range control that announces current, minimum and maximum values.
ariaPatterns.tablist: Tab strip with keyboard navigation and associated content panels.
ariaPatterns.live: Polite live region for dynamic announcements to screen readers.
ariaPatterns.progressbar: Progress indicator with numeric value, minimum and maximum.
ariaPatterns.tooltip: Contextual description linked to the triggering element via aria-describedby.

testDesc: Recommended tools and a basic checklist to verify accessibility in your application.

tools.axe: Open source JS engine. Integrate in Jest/Vitest to catch issues in CI/CD pipelines.
tools.axeExt: Chrome & Firefox extension. Run on-demand audits directly in the browser DevTools.
tools.wave: Visual overlay tool. Highlights errors, alerts and structure issues on the page in real time.
tools.lighthouse: Built into Chrome DevTools. Full accessibility audit alongside performance and SEO.
tools.voiceover: Native macOS/iOS screen reader. No install needed — press Cmd+F5 to start.
tools.nvda: Free open source Windows screen reader. Most widely used with Chrome and Firefox.

checklist.item1: All interactive elements are reachable and operable by keyboard alone.
checklist.item2: Focus indicator is always visible — never hidden with outline: none.
checklist.item3: No unintentional keyboard traps outside of modal overlays.
checklist.item4: Text color contrast meets 4.5:1 (normal text) or 3:1 (large text and UI components).
checklist.item5: Non-decorative images have a meaningful alt attribute.
checklist.item6: Dynamic state changes (open, selected, error) are announced to screen readers.
checklist.item7: Page remains fully usable at 200% browser zoom without horizontal scrolling.
checklist.item8: Error messages are programmatically linked to their inputs via aria-describedby.
